top_signals.title: Law enforcement groups warn Clarity Act could hinder crypto crime investigations
top_signals.summary: A provision in the Clarity Act could make it harder for law enforcement to investigate illicit crypto activity, the groups said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Germany scraps plans to build its biggest warship since second world war
top_signals.summary: Shares in defence group Rheinmetall tumble as Berlin ditches F126 frigate programme after cost overruns and delays
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnham coronation closer yet Starmer ally raises alarm on borrowing
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham’s path to Downing Street has been all but cleared after Darren Jones ruled out entering a leadership race, spilling over to further speculation over the look of the next government’s Cabinet.  Jones, a close ally of Sir Keir Starmer, was rumoured as a potential challenger to Burnham but the chief secretary to the [...]

top_signals.title: Korea’s Surging Equity Volatility Draws Meme-Stock Comparisons
top_signals.summary: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

top_signals.title: Is this teenage girl North Korea’s next dictator?
top_signals.summary: Emergence of Kim Jong Un’s daughter at many public events fuels speculation that she is being lined up as a leader

top_signals.title: Commission strengthens Europol and Eurojust to step up the fight against cross-border crime and terrorism
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 24 Jun 2026 Today, the Commission is proposing new measures to strengthen the EU's response to an evolving criminal landscape. Crime is becoming more sophisticated, international and digital. To tackle serious crime effectively, police, customs, prosecutors and courts need to work closely together from the start of an investigation to the final court judgment.
